name: therapy_scheduling_strings
description: Regression test for the 'grounding is not safe' bug: aggregate rules whose bodies contain anonymous variables (_), assignment aggregates (DUR = #sum{...}) and string constants must be instrumentable, and explaining a literal must not crash.
program:
x(RID,DAY,TS) : mss(DAY,TS) :- reg(RID,_).
preparation_time(RID,DUR) :- reg(RID,_), DUR = #sum{D,PH: duration(RID,PH,D), PH != 4}.
:- x(RID,_,TS), reg(RID,_), preparation_time(RID,T), TS-T < 1.
patient_seat_day(ID,T,RID,DAY) : seat(ID,T) :- x(RID,DAY,_), reg(RID,T).
patient_seat_ts(ID,T,RID,DAY,TS..TS+DUR-1) :- patient_seat_day(ID,T,RID,DAY), x(RID,DAY,TS), duration(RID,4,DUR), DUR > 0.
:- #count{RID: patient_seat_ts(ID,T,RID,DAY,TS)} > 1, seat(ID,T), mss(DAY, TS).
reg("pat1","bed").
reg("pat2","chair").
reg("pat3","chair").
mss(1,1).
mss(1,2).
mss(1,3).
mss(1,4).
mss(1,5).
mss(1,6).
mss(1,7).
mss(1,8).
mss(1,9).
duration("pat1",4,5).
duration("pat1",1,2).
duration("pat2",4,3).
duration("pat2",3,1).
duration("pat2",1,2).
duration("pat3",4,4).
duration("pat3",3,2).
duration("pat3",1,2).
seat("bed1","bed").
seat("chair1","chair").
seat("chair2","chair").

explanations.comment: No expected_responses: the test only verifies that the explanation runs without the unsafe-grounding error.
